description: Synthetic waiting-room check-ins at T+minutes after MCI declaration: quick-registration aliases for unknowns, partial identities for the panel patients who arrive with the incident, and the ordinary walk-ins who never stop coming.

arrivals.name: TRAUMA, ALPHA (unknown male)
arrivals.complaint: Blast injury — self-transported, no EMS tag
arrivals.note: Pt 31: adult male, ambulatory to cot then unable to continue. RR 38, radial pulse present, follows commands, no external bleeding. No other assessment documented yet.

arrivals.name: WILKERSON?, LATOYIA (partial ID)
arrivals.complaint: Head strike, elderly — daughter reports blood thinner
arrivals.note: Pt 14: elderly female pulled from the collapsed section, brought in by a bystander's truck. States her name is Latoyia, last name sounded like 'Wilkerson' — daughter says she takes a blood thinner and a heart pill. Struck head on debris, GCS now improving, follows commands, breathing without distress, radial pulse present, scalp laceration bleeding controlled. Looks about 80.

arrivals.name: TRAUMA, BRAVO (male ~80)
arrivals.complaint: Confused elderly male, medical alert bracelet
arrivals.note: Pt 19: male, roughly 80, oriented x1, cannot state his own name reliably. Neighbor at the scene thought his name was something like 'Masikovich' or 'Majkovic'. Medical alert bracelet notes a blood thinner. Breathing, follows simple commands, radial pulse present, no respiratory distress, no external bleeding seen.

arrivals.name: TRAUMA, CHARLIE (plant worker)
arrivals.complaint: Chemical exposure — decon done at scene
arrivals.note: Pt 41: worker from the plant floor, decon completed at scene. Pinpoint pupils, drooling, sweating heavily, muscle fasciculations. RR 34, radial pulse present, follows commands, no external bleeding. Name unknown.

arrivals.name: TRAUMA, DELTA (facial trauma)
arrivals.complaint: Debris strike to face, vision fading left eye
arrivals.note: Pt 44: adult male struck in the face by debris. Left eye proptotic with a rock-hard tense orbit, vision going dark in that eye. Breathing comfortably, follows commands, radial pulse present, facial bleeding controlled with pressure.

arrivals.name: TRAUMA, ECHO (no assessment)
arrivals.complaint: Dropped at door, crews pulled away
arrivals.note: Pt 23: adult, breathing. Crews pulled to the next bay before any further assessment. No identifying information.

arrivals.name: PORTER, DANIELLE
arrivals.complaint: Twisted ankle at home — unrelated walk-in
arrivals.note: Walk-in during the MCI: adult female, inversion ankle injury at home this morning. Ambulatory with a limp, breathing comfortably, follows commands, radial pulse present, no bleeding. Minor injury only.

arrivals.name: OKAFOR, SAMUEL
arrivals.complaint: Chest tightness since this morning — walk-in
arrivals.note: Walk-in during the MCI: middle-aged male with intermittent chest tightness since breakfast, now resolved. Walking, talking in full sentences, breathing comfortably, follows commands, radial pulse strong, no bleeding. Injuries: none — medical complaint.
